com.pax.poslink.peripheries.tech

Class MifareUltralight

  • java.lang.Object
    • BasicTechnology
      • com.pax.poslink.peripheries.tech.MifareUltralight

  • public class MifareUltralight
extends BasicTechnology
    Provides access to MIFARE Ultralight properties and I/O operations on a CardInfo.

    Acquire a MifareUltralight object using get(com.pax.poslink.peripheries.tech.CardInfo).

    MIFARE Ultralight compatible have 4 byte pages PAGE_SIZE. The primary operations on an Ultralight tag are readPages(int) and writePage(int, byte[]).

    The original MIFARE Ultralight consists of a 64 byte EEPROM. The first 4 pages are for the OTP area, manufacturer data, and locking bits. They are readable and some bits are writable. The final 12 pages are the user read/write area. For more information see the NXP data sheet MF0ICU1.

    The MIFARE Ultralight C consists of a 192 byte EEPROM. The first 4 pages are for OTP, manufacturer data, and locking bits. The next 36 pages are the user read/write area. The next 4 pages are additional locking bits, counters and authentication configuration and are readable. The final 4 pages are for the authentication key and are not readable. For more information see the NXP data sheet MF0ICU2.

    The MIFARE Ultralight EV1 consists of 640-bit or 1312-bit, organized in 20 or 41 pages with 4 bytes per page. For more information see the NXP data sheet MF0ULX1.

    Note: Methods that perform I/O operations require the "com.pax.permission.PICC" permission.

    Since:
    V1.04.00

    • Method Detail

      • get

        public static MifareUltralight get(CardInfo cardInfo)
        Get an instance of MifareUltralight for the given cardInfo.

        If the card is not a MifareUltralight, it will returns null.

        Parameters:
        cardInfo - the card info
        Returns:
        MIFARE Ultralight object

      • authentication

        public boolean authentication(byte[] key)
                       throws PiccException
        Only supported if the card is TYPE_ULTRALIGHT_C or TYPE_ULTRALIGHT_EV1

        For TYPE_ULTRALIGHT_EV1 :
        The execution is the PWD_AUTH command. A protected memory area can be accessed only after a successful password verification using the PWD_AUTH command.
        For TYPE_ULTRALIGHT_C :
        Performed 3DES Authentication.

        Parameters:
        key - the key
        Returns:
        true on success, false on authentication failure
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • getType

        public int getType()
        Return the MIFARE Ultralight type.

        One of TYPE_ULTRALIGHT or TYPE_ULTRALIGHT_C or TYPE_ULTRALIGHT_EV1 or TYPE_UNKNOWN.

        Does not cause any RF activity and does not block.

        Returns:
        the type

      • readPages

        public byte[] readPages(int pageOffset)
                 throws PiccException
        Read 4 pages (16 bytes).

        The MIFARE Ultralight protocol always reads 4 pages at a time, to reduce the number of commands required to read an entire tag.

        If a read spans past the last readable block, then the tag will return pages that have been wrapped back to the first blocks. MIFARE Ultralight tags have readable blocks 0x00 through 0x0F. So a read to block offset 0x0E would return blocks 0x0E, 0x0F, 0x00, 0x01. MIFARE Ultralight C tags have readable blocks 0x00 through 0x2B. So a read to block 0x2A would return blocks 0x2A, 0x2B, 0x00, 0x01.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        pageOffset - index of first page to read, starting from 0
        Returns:
        4 pages (16 bytes)
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • writePage

        public void writePage(int pageOffset,
                      byte[] data)
               throws PiccException
        Write 1 page (4 bytes).

        The MIFARE Ultralight protocol always writes 1 page at a time, to minimize EEPROM write cycles.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        pageOffset - index of page to write, starting from 0
        data - 4 bytes to write
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • transceive

        public byte[] transceive(byte[] data)
                  throws PiccException
        Send raw data to the card and receive the response.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Parameters:
        data - bytes to send
        Returns:
        bytes received in response
        Throws:
        PiccException

      • transceive

        public byte[] transceive(byte[] data,
                         byte mode)
                  throws PiccException
        Send raw data to the card and receive the response.

        This is an I/O operation and will block until complete. It must not be called from the main application thread.

        Mode:
        Bit0 = 0, means sending disabled CRC, Bit0 = 1, means sending enabled CRC.
        Bit1 = 0, means that the CRC is disabled for reception, Bit0 = 1, means that the CRC is enabled for reception.
        Bit2 ~ Bit7 will be used in the future, currently set to 0

        Parameters:
        data - bytes to send
        mode - mode of transceive
        Returns:
        bytes received in response
        Throws:
        PiccException
